[2018-03-16 21:23] http404error: We're arguing over how exactly SDR is defined though
[2018-03-16 21:23] http404error: You can't just say "Everything that sounds like it should be good should dominate"
[2018-03-16 21:23] http404error: You can't even say "Everything that usually is a good thing should dominate"
[2018-03-16 21:23] mqp: bowing out, this conversation is getting pretty bad
[2018-03-16 21:23] http404error: You have to define what an "improved status" is to be able to apply that bullet point in the general case
[2018-03-16 21:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): would you call low buildtime SDR breaking
[2018-03-16 21:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): >Units with improved statuses should be better than units with worse ones
[2018-03-16 21:25] http404error: Why do you consider Prompt to be an improved status?
[2018-03-16 21:25] http404error: Because it's usually a good thing?
[2018-03-16 21:26] http404error: Because it sounds like it should be a good thing?
[2018-03-16 21:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): which should be better than bt1
[2018-03-16 21:26] http404error: Why do you think bt should be a dominating factor?
[2018-03-16 21:26] namington: I interpreted "improved statuses" as applying to things such as different Stamina amounts, different amounts of health on a Fragile unit
[2018-03-16 21:26] namington: I dont think that low buildtime positions *always* dominate over high buildtime positions
[2018-03-16 21:26] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Not for lifespan attackers or Frontline attackers :P
[2018-03-16 21:26] namington: that isnt to say that having low buildtime is SDR breaking
[2018-03-16 21:26] namington: because that's not how SDR works
[2018-03-16 21:27] namington: but something that lowered the buildtime of your other units
[2018-03-16 21:27] namington: would probably break SDR
[2018-03-16 21:27] http404error: if it was mandatory, yes
[2018-03-16 21:27] namington: (even though the cases where it actually matters would be very few-and-far-between)
[2018-03-16 21:27] Deleted User (Deleted User): > (e.g. units with higher hp should be better than identical units with lower hp; units with higher remaining lifespan should be better than identical units with lower remaining lifespan, etc..)
[2018-03-16 21:27] http404error: Yes, there is a strictly defined set of "improved statuses"
[2018-03-16 21:27] http404error: It's not exhaustively listed in the article
[2018-03-16 21:28] http404error: Prompt is not included in that set
[2018-03-16 21:28] http404error: Neither is buildtime (for normal purchases)
[2018-03-16 21:29] .bky_1556 (bky): As far as statuses go, normal blocking > prompt blocking > exhausted.
[2018-03-16 21:29] http404error: Why do you say that?
[2018-03-16 21:29] .bky_1556 (bky): For the purpose of SDR.
[2018-03-16 21:29] liadahlia (Lia): how does exhaust breaks SDR?
[2018-03-16 21:30] http404error: Exhaustedness is definitely a strictly bad thing
[2018-03-16 21:30] .bky_1556 (bky): You would always rather have a unit in a blocking state than exhausted.
[2018-03-16 21:31] http404error: Blocker in general dominates
[2018-03-16 21:31] http404error: Okay, there might be a countercase
[2018-03-16 21:33] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I'm pretty sure blocker is an improved status over non-blockers
[2018-03-16 21:34] http404error: That's what I thought too... I'm questioning myself though
[2018-03-16 21:34] liadahlia (Lia): normal blocking doesn't dominate prompt blocking though
[2018-03-16 21:34] namington: independent of promptness, I believe it is?
[2018-03-16 21:34] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Nope, it's weaker vs Breach.
[2018-03-16 21:34] namington: it depends on you being able to
[2018-03-16 21:34] namington: click to exhaust that unit
[2018-03-16 21:35] .bky_1556 (bky): Prompt doesn't dominate vs. Gold (i.e. constructing), but that *doesn't matter* because you never have both states on the same unit.
[2018-03-16 21:35] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): While non-blocker can hang back.
[2018-03-16 21:35] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): If you are in a situation where it dies while blocking, compare that to them breaching for the same amount and killing it anyway
[2018-03-16 21:35] namington: yeah, I believe that, assuming no unit has prompt
[2018-03-16 21:35] namington: a unit with blocker dominates the same unit without blocker
[2018-03-16 21:36] namington: I cant think of a clear counterexample
[2018-03-16 21:36] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): No, because they lost power on Blockers.
[2018-03-16 21:36] http404error: I can't come up with the counterexample either
[2018-03-16 21:36] http404error: I was trying to come up with something tricky with Iceblade and Borehole Patroller

[2018-03-16 21:36] liadahlia (Lia): prompt might not dominate gold armor but gold armor also doesn't dominate prompt
[2018-03-16 21:37] http404error: Just like neither 2 Husks nor 1 Wall dominate each other

[2018-03-16 21:38] http404error: Nor does it mean that Wall *should* dominate just because it's usually better, or seems like it should be better.
[2018-03-16 21:38] http404error: That case is intentionally excluded from SDR to provide additional depth to the game

[2018-03-16 21:47] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Blocker dominates because Enemy doesn't get to Spread damage himself.
[2018-03-16 21:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): punf and I were discussing this a while back

[2018-03-16 21:48] http404error: Including in discussions of domination

[2018-03-16 21:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): greater potential for misplays

[2018-03-16 21:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): a blocker fang is close
[2018-03-16 21:49] http404error: Okay, we hear what you're saying
[2018-03-16 21:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): we come to the conclusion(me and punf) that blocker fang is ~= to current
[2018-03-16 21:49] http404error: But we already disclaimed why it's not factoring into our discussion of SDR

[2018-03-16 21:50] liadahlia (Lia): because there *are* situations where you'd rather block with your luci
[2018-03-16 21:50] liadahlia (Lia): might be unthinkable but hey, it exists
[2018-03-16 21:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): So I guess you can't be bothered including taking more time/harder to play eh
[2018-03-16 21:51] f300xen (f0x): isn't blocker always strictly better than not having it assuming a spherical perfect player in a vacuum

[2018-03-16 21:51] http404error: We're intentionally and consciously not accounting for potential for misplays or time usage.

--- Thread (43 messages, 2018-03-16) ---
[2018-03-16 21:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): For the record, it's probs sounding a bit off topic but eh
[2018-03-16 21:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): if they made luci a blocker, I would consider it a nerf
[2018-03-16 21:53] cale: @f0x yes, having blocker is always better than not having blocker
[2018-03-16 21:53] liadahlia (Lia): but well, technically it's strictly better
[2018-03-16 21:53] liadahlia (Lia): but practically it's just annoying
[2018-03-16 21:53] cale: yeah, the UI is worse if you have blockers that you don't want to lose
[2018-03-16 21:53] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Attack to kill Blocker|Non: Other Blocker HP + Unit HP.
[2018-03-16 21:53] liadahlia (Lia): defending with boreholes or xeno on the field is super annoying
[2018-03-16 21:53] liadahlia (Lia): or resophore forcefields
[2018-03-16 21:54] http404error: Oh, yeah, I hate the extra math
[2018-03-16 21:54] liadahlia (Lia): i remember elyot talked about adding an ability to "unblock" unit
[2018-03-16 21:54] cale: but... there almost ought to be a way to mark units as not part of your defence, even if you don't want to click them
[2018-03-16 21:54] liadahlia (Lia): but well, seems like that never happened
[2018-03-16 21:54] cale: just to make the totals more appropriate
[2018-03-16 21:54] cale: yeah, it's sort of complicated
[2018-03-16 21:54] http404error: That would be interesting, but I think it might lead to more mistakes than it prevents!
[2018-03-16 21:54] cale: The UI is already quite complicated
[2018-03-16 21:55] wiwiweb: I want something like this: https://www.reddit.com/r/Prismata/comments/83tjid/what_would_it_take_to_make_the_breach_indicator/dvkyb06/
What would it take to make the breach indicator perfectly figure o...
I have a midway idea that doesn't take as much technical work and still keeps a little of the problem solving on the player. What if you could...
[2018-03-16 21:55] http404error: "Oops, accidentally clicked my Wall/Forcefield/whatever"
[2018-03-16 21:55] liadahlia (Lia): yeah ctrl click to subtract it from your defense makes sense
[2018-03-16 21:55] cale: @Wiwiweb but this is even one step beyond that
[2018-03-16 21:55] liadahlia (Lia): it's just a little bit janky
[2018-03-16 21:55] cale: you're not taking a breach, but you're losing blockers that you don't want to lose, which is as bad as being breached (in some cases)
[2018-03-16 21:57] cale: also, then you'd have blocker and unclicked vs. clicked vs. not defending
[2018-03-16 21:57] cale: so you have to make those visually distinct
[2018-03-16 21:58] cale: and do you actually make the not-defending units not part of the defence, or just not part of the total (I was thinking the latter...)
[2018-03-16 21:58] cale: But people might get confused about that when their opponent does it...
[2018-03-16 21:59] http404error: I'd assume you just don't change the counter for your opponent
[2018-03-16 21:59] liadahlia (Lia): i think the not-defending unit should still be part of the defense
[2018-03-16 21:59] http404error: It's a self-aid only
[2018-03-16 21:59] wiwiweb: the visual could be entirely on your side
[2018-03-16 21:59] liadahlia (Lia): unclicking is just self-aid
[2018-03-16 21:59] wiwiweb: it would only affect the attack defense sum numbers
[2018-03-16 22:00] http404error: Anyways, to bring it all back around
[2018-03-16 22:06] http404error: "Breaking SDR" for the challenge should involve creating situations where either player might wish:
* Their units had less hp.
* They didn't have a unit that they have.
* They had less resources, attack, or supply seeds.
* Their units didn't have Blocker, or had less lifespan or stamina remaining.
* Their units had Fragile or were Exhausted or Chilled.
[2018-03-16 22:08] http404error: It doesn't count to only make situations where either player might wish:
* Their units had less buildtime, or no invulnerability
* A unit they purchased didn't have Prompt
* They had a Drone instead of a Vivid Drone
because all these cases already exist in the game in some way.
[2018-03-16 22:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): a conduit with less hp is a buff in bp against master bot or Q defenders I think
[2018-03-16 22:09] http404error: Haha, yes. Don't account for the game clock or for mistakes when considering the above.
[2018-03-16 22:11] Deleted User (Deleted User): I really want to be obnoxious here but I find it fucking hilarious, punf claims nivo being 4 chill and vai being 6 is a buff(because people will underuse it less)
[2018-03-16 22:11] http404error: Those are entertaining claims
[2018-03-16 22:11] http404error: I'm not bothered by considering them 😛
[2018-03-16 22:14] http404error: I'm wondering if I've missed any cases for SDR
[2018-03-16 22:16] http404error: The negative cases are impossible to enumerate completely in any reasonable fashion, of course

messages.author: http404error
messages.content:
"Breaking SDR" for the challenge should involve creating situations where either player might wish:
* Their units had less hp.
* They didn't have a unit that they have.
* They had less resources, attack, or supply seeds.
* Their units didn't have Blocker, or had less lifespan or stamina remaining.
* Their units had Fragile or were Exhausted or Chilled.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content:
It doesn't count to only make situations where either player might wish:
* Their units had less buildtime, or no invulnerability
* A unit they purchased didn't have Prompt
* They had a Drone instead of a Vivid Drone
because all these cases already exist in the game in some way.

[2018-03-17 02:54] kaelii: You take 3 on your blastocyst and then click for 4 extra prompt health and you've gotten 7 damage absorbed, gave your opponent 4 damage to absorb and then you get 4 MORE health back.
[2018-03-17 02:56] kaelii: But if you can just click your blastocyst without it getting damaged you get 10 health over a few turns, and give them 4 if you kill it efficiently.
[2018-03-17 02:58] kaelii: Unfortunately it probably also falls under the "if you can do this, there is almost certainly a better way to win harder anyway"
[2018-03-17 03:06] liadahlia (Lia): Oooh so the spawn gains the health that is missing
[2018-03-17 03:07] liadahlia (Lia): I thought the spawn only has one health
[2018-03-17 03:07] liadahlia (Lia): So you're clicking to gain granularity or something
[2018-03-17 03:08] liadahlia (Lia): So basically it is only ever good in the early game
[2018-03-17 03:08] liadahlia (Lia): And also very snowbally
[2018-03-17 03:08] liadahlia (Lia): How does frontline works with fragile anyways
[2018-03-17 03:08] f300xen (f0x): there are no frontline fragile units
[2018-03-17 03:09] f300xen (f0x): but i assume you just assign all possible damage when you click it
[2018-03-17 03:10] liadahlia (Lia): When it has 2 health, you click it and gain a 2 health prompt? It will then become 1 health?
[2018-03-17 03:10] liadahlia (Lia): And then your last click spawns a 4 health?
[2018-03-17 03:12] liadahlia (Lia): Anyways when the opponent has more than 4 atk you would never buy it
[2018-03-17 03:13] liadahlia (Lia): It's certainly interesting
[2018-03-17 03:14] kaelii: If it is at 2, and you click it goes to 1, and you get a 3 health prompt.
[2018-03-17 03:14] liadahlia (Lia): What if you click it at 1 health?
[2018-03-17 03:14] kaelii: Then you get a 4 health prompt, your thing dies, your opponent gets the thing winding up.
[2018-03-17 03:15] liadahlia (Lia): Also this unit lead to potentially snowbally situations
[2018-03-17 03:15] kaelii: And your 4 health prompt is doomed so can't pile up a bunch of those spawned things unless you're buying more than one of these and then we go down crazy town for a few turns.
[2018-03-17 03:15] liadahlia (Lia): Which might turn out to be degenerate
[2018-03-17 03:16] kaelii: And eventually you both run out of blastocysts.
[2018-03-17 03:16] liadahlia (Lia): A green fragile absorber
[2018-03-17 03:17] kaelii: It's not a great at absorbing damage if it dies immediately, but if it takes damage and you can click it (first click doesn't do much besides a granularity), then it starts offering loads of shenanigans.
[2018-03-17 03:18] liadahlia (Lia): I know this takes it out of the contest, but it would be a *really* interesting unit if it cost more, doesn't have the death effect, and is not frontline
[2018-03-17 03:18] liadahlia (Lia): Basically a green absorber that requires a lot of finesse
[2018-03-17 03:18] kaelii: Oh yeah no, it definitely needs a whole pile of fixing to be anywhere near sane.
[2018-03-17 03:20] liadahlia (Lia): I really like the idea of a click ability that gets prompt block for every health missing
[2018-03-17 03:24] kaelii: Definitely can do some work on that alone, tuning cost/supply/amount of health each can possibly generate/ease in capitalizing on said health would probably be a big task.
[2018-03-17 03:25] liadahlia (Lia): I think it's interesting but the fact that you can regain it after yours die by killing the opponent's can lead to snowball situation
[2018-03-17 03:26] liadahlia (Lia): For example you rush Redeemer and an attack and get this unit before he even get any attack
[2018-03-17 03:26] liadahlia (Lia): When he reaches 3 attack
[2018-03-17 03:26] liadahlia (Lia): And then you click it and kill his on the next turn
[2018-03-17 03:27] liadahlia (Lia): While putting in so much pressure that he can't get up more attackers
[2018-03-17 03:27] liadahlia (Lia): Now that I wrote that out that's very unlikely to happen tho
[2018-03-17 03:29] kaelii: I mean, yeah if it is frontline it is REALLY easy for the two of you to just trade them back and forth. It might even still be playable against some of the periodic attackers.
[2018-03-17 03:29] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Having variable health and attack and spawning things for your opponent is a lot to manage.
[2018-03-17 03:30] kaelii: It is an absolute beautiful mess. Don't you :Fragile: it?
[2018-03-17 03:38] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): No? It's like DotA 2 techies, technically it can exist within the game's framework, but it is so warping and out of place that it shouldn't.

messages.content: Anyways when the opponent has more than 4 atk you would never buy it

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: It's certainly interesting

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: If it is at 2, and you click it goes to 1, and you get a 3 health prompt.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: What if you click it at 1 health?

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: Then you get a 4 health prompt, your thing dies, your opponent gets the thing winding up.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: Also this unit lead to potentially snowbally situations

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: And your 4 health prompt is doomed so can't pile up a bunch of those spawned things unless you're buying more than one of these and then we go down crazy town for a few turns.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: Which might turn out to be degenerate

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: And eventually you both run out of blastocysts.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: A green fragile absorber

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: It's not a great at absorbing damage if it dies immediately, but if it takes damage and you can click it (first click doesn't do much besides a granularity), then it starts offering loads of shenanigans.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: I know this takes it out of the contest, but it would be a *really* interesting unit if it cost more, doesn't have the death effect, and is not frontline

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: Basically a green absorber that requires a lot of finesse

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: Oh yeah no, it definitely needs a whole pile of fixing to be anywhere near sane.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: I really like the idea of a click ability that gets prompt block for every health missing

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: Definitely can do some work on that alone, tuning cost/supply/amount of health each can possibly generate/ease in capitalizing on said health would probably be a big task.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: I think it's interesting but the fact that you can regain it after yours die by killing the opponent's can lead to snowball situation

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: For example you rush Redeemer and an attack and get this unit before he even get any attack

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: When he reaches 3 attack

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: And then you click it and kill his on the next turn

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: While putting in so much pressure that he can't get up more attackers

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: Now that I wrote that out that's very unlikely to happen tho

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: I mean, yeah if it is frontline it is REALLY easy for the two of you to just trade them back and forth. It might even still be playable against some of the periodic attackers.

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: Having variable health and attack and spawning things for your opponent is a lot to manage.

messages.author: kaelii
messages.content: It is an absolute beautiful mess. Don't you :Fragile: it?

messages.author: randomflyingtaco
messages.content: No? It's like DotA 2 techies, technically it can exist within the game's framework, but it is so warping and out of place that it shouldn't.

[2018-03-17 03:50] kaelii: I'm curious where you think it seriously warps the game? Giving your opponent a copy of the unit when it dies?
[2018-03-17 03:51] http404error: @Kaelii Interesting uniti
[2018-03-17 03:51] http404error: Definitely qualifies for the challenge btw
[2018-03-17 03:51] http404error: To answer your question on reddit
[2018-03-17 03:51] http404error: I think the click ability is maybe too complex though. Does the "copy" have the death trigger ability? It feels like there should be some way to simplify that part of the unit greatly.
[2018-03-17 03:53] kaelii: The click spawned unit is just health, while the unit your opponent gets for killing yours should be more or less functionally the same as the supply unit you built.
[2018-03-17 03:54] http404error: Why not just make that many Husks then, or something?
[2018-03-17 03:55] kaelii: It certainly could click-spawn husks, but I feel that would be a buff against freeze and apollo?
[2018-03-17 04:00] http404error: @Kaelii I think simplifying the unit's most complex and confusing effect is a good place to start regardless
[2018-03-17 04:00] http404error: Tracking a big stack of varying fragile health sounds annoying
[2018-03-17 04:01] http404error: Also they have to be a separate stack if it's not technically the same unit
[2018-03-17 04:01] http404error: Also being Frontline only complicates it further
[2018-03-17 04:03] http404error: I guess, what's the goal of that ability? To make you want to have it damaged?
[2018-03-17 04:04] http404error: That and deathrattle are both SDR violations - you don't necessarily need to have both, and you don't get extra credit for breaking SDR in two ways
[2018-03-17 04:06] kaelii: I mean, the idea is similar to it having 4 stamina for clicks
[2018-03-17 04:09] kaelii: Generally you want to click them instead of letting them take damage, as the click is always 1 health but gives more health on later clicks.
[2018-03-17 04:10] kaelii: So they CAN block for 4 now, or they can block for up to 10 over a few turns if you can use the health effectively.
[2018-03-17 04:12] http404error: I can see why Frontline is important
[2018-03-17 04:12] http404error: It still seems too complex to be worth it though, that's my advice.
[2018-03-17 04:13] kaelii: Yeah if your opponent CAN'T target them, then they are probably doomed with just a 2-3 hp immediate blocker on them.
[2018-03-17 04:15] kaelii: But then they end up being a kind of generic blocker that probably fits better under blue.
[2018-03-17 04:16] liadahlia (Lia): if your opponent can't target them then you would just click it for 10 health over 4 turns
[2018-03-17 04:16] liadahlia (Lia): 3 green for 10 health is ridiculous
[2018-03-17 04:16] kaelii: Or click it for 6 health, keep it at 1 if it still has an attack.
[2018-03-17 04:17] liadahlia (Lia): 6 health for GGGRR is pretty nutty
[2018-03-17 04:18] kaelii: I mean, it IS a lot of tech to be spending on health.
[2018-03-17 04:18] kaelii: Good luck buying two in a turn.
[2018-03-17 04:19] liadahlia (Lia): it's not that expensive
[2018-03-17 04:19] liadahlia (Lia): 6 gold's worth of tech?
[2018-03-17 04:21] http404error: It's definitely worth teching up for, if that's the case
[2018-03-17 04:22] liadahlia (Lia): i'd still be incline to buy this even if it's GGGRRRR
[2018-03-17 04:22] http404error: Hmm... maybe it doesn't even need to make attack, and just be a really weird blocker?
[2018-03-17 04:22] http404error: I think removing the attack makes reasoning about the unit strategically much easier
[2018-03-17 04:23] liadahlia (Lia): anyways i like the unit a lot, i'm just not liking the deathrattle effect
[2018-03-17 04:25] kaelii: I mean, I did originally start it out under the concept of a R+G unit that blocks fairly well.
[2018-03-17 04:26] http404error: I like that take, it's a bit unusual for red-green
[2018-03-17 04:26] http404error: Even Protoplasm is a decidedly offensive blocker.
[2018-03-17 04:27] kaelii: It could easily be implemented as just "Blocker-Dork, X stamina, click for doomed blocker"
[2018-03-17 04:28] kaelii: Promptness, fragile/health quantities up for details.
[2018-03-17 04:29] liadahlia (Lia): ok how bout this version: 6GGGRR 4 fragile prompt blocker. When this unit takes damage and survive, spawn a copy of this unit with as much health as the damage taken.
[2018-03-17 04:29] liadahlia (Lia): the spawn is prompt so it will block for the turn it spawns
[2018-03-17 04:30] liadahlia (Lia): the spawn also has the same ability
[2018-03-17 04:30] liadahlia (Lia): so effectively you have a decaying absorber
[2018-03-17 04:30] liadahlia (Lia): on second thought this unit will need to be cheaper because it's not really absorbing anything
[2018-03-17 04:31] liadahlia (Lia): anyways effectively providing 10 health over 4 turns
[2018-03-17 04:32] kaelii: Not quite, you have to control so that you deal 3-2-1 to it. If you deal 1, it spawns a 1.
[2018-03-17 04:32] http404error: I like the idea of a decaying absorber which makes you massage your defense thresholds
[2018-03-17 04:33] http404error: But it seems like you can treat it as a 4hp nonfragile blocker in practice a lot of the time
[2018-03-17 04:33] liadahlia (Lia): yeah this unit kinda sucks looking at it
[2018-03-17 04:33] liadahlia (Lia): maybe 5 health or 6 health to begin with

[2018-03-17 05:38] http404error: "Take something that is currently Always True in the game of Prismata, and break it so that it is no longer Always True."
[2018-03-17 05:38] http404error: another way of looking at breaking SDR
[2018-03-17 05:39] http404error: 10 shame points to whoever says their unit breaks the claim "There is no unit named ___ in Prismata"
[2018-03-17 05:40] http404error: There are some really interesting gray areas exposed by this!
[2018-03-17 05:41] http404error: It is currently Always True in Prismata that you'd rather lose a Wall than a Steelsplitter. But if there was ever a unit created that required you to sacrifice a Wall to build it, that would no longer be Always True.
[2018-03-17 05:41] http404error: I'd argue that SDR does *not* try to claim that losing a Wall should always be better than losing a Steelsplitter, so that reveals a significant flaw in my simplified explanation in the quote above
